probedServerVersion: Sourcegraph Enterprise (probed 2026-04-08; streaming lab probed 2026-05-05 on anthropic::2024-10-22::claude-sonnet-4-5-latest)
description: Sourcegraph Enterprise LLM and model-catalog HTTP surfaces used by the plugin. Two distinct wire shapes: (1) OpenAI-compatible REST at /.api/llm/* for text-only agent turns; (2) Cody-shape SSE at /.api/completions/stream for image-bearing turns. Additional catalog endpoints (/.api/modelconfig/supported-models.json, /.api/client-config) drive per-model context-window sizing and api-version negotiation. Auth is always 'Authorization: token <TOKEN>' — not Bearer. All clients are constructed in isolation (no HttpClientFactory shared pool) per project_sourcegraph_isolation.md.

endpoints.summary: OpenAI-compatible streaming and non-streaming chat completions. Used for all text-only agent turns (BrainRouter routes text-only here; image-bearing turns route to /.api/completions/stream instead). As of 2026-05-13 XML-in-content migration, tools are NOT sent as a request parameter — tool definitions live in the system prompt only, and the LLM emits tool calls as XML in assistant text content.

params.name: Authorization
params.description: Sourcegraph token auth. Literal prefix 'token' (not Bearer). Emitted automatically by AuthInterceptor(AuthScheme.TOKEN).

endpoints.requestBody: ChatCompletionRequest JSON: {model (required, format provider::apiVersion::modelId), messages (OpenAI-compatible, system role remapped to user with <system_instructions> tags by MessageSanitizer), max_tokens (optional; MANDATORY for thinking models — omitting causes HTTP 500), temperature (default 0.0), stream (boolean), stream_options: {include_usage: true} when streaming}. NOTE: tools field is always null in production (XML-in-content migration 2026-05-13). tool_choice is NOT sent (unsupported by API). Consecutive same-role messages are merged by MessageSanitizer before sending.
endpoints.provenance: SourcegraphChatClient.kt:79 (CHAT_COMPLETIONS_PATH constant), SourcegraphChatClient.kt:36-50 (KDoc), raw_sse/text_only_A.txt + single_tool_A.txt (streaming lab), research/2026-04-08-sourcegraph-api-surfaces.md Surface 1

verdict.classification: R-CORE: primary LLM inference endpoint for all text-only agent turns
verdict.reasoning: All non-image agent turns go through this endpoint via OpenAiCompatBrain. Post 2026-05-13 migration tools are XML-in-system-prompt; native tool_calls frames are still emitted by the server (probe confirmed) but are no longer requested and are parsed by AssistantMessageParser from raw SSE text.

gotchas: Auth prefix is 'token' (not 'Bearer'). AuthInterceptor(AuthScheme.TOKEN) emits 'Authorization: token <value>' — confirmed in AuthInterceptor.kt:24.
model field uses triple-colon format: provider::apiVersion::modelId (e.g. anthropic::2024-10-22::claude-sonnet-4-5-latest). Confirmed in streaming_lab_results.json and ModelInfo.kt KDoc.
max_tokens is MANDATORY for thinking models — omitting causes HTTP 500. Limit varies per model; probe confirmed 8K/16K/100K accepted. Agent sends up to 64K successfully.

tool_choice is NOT supported by the Sourcegraph API. The field is accepted for interface compatibility in sendMessage() but is always set to null before serialization (SourcegraphChatClient.kt:459 toolChoice=null).

tools field is always null on the wire since XML-in-content migration 2026-05-13. Tool definitions live in system prompt only. Native tool_calls SSE delta frames are still emitted by the server if tools were declared, but the plugin never declares them.
stream: true uses SSE on the same endpoint. Sourcegraph's spec labels stream as 'Unsupported' but it works in practice (confirmed probe + research/2026-04-08-sourcegraph-api-surfaces.md).
response object field value is the literal string 'object' (not 'chat.completion' like upstream OpenAI). Confirmed in SourcegraphChatClient.kt:48.
tool role messages are not in the Sourcegraph spec. MessageSanitizer remaps tool-role turns to user with plain-text prefix 'RESULT of {toolName}:' before sending.
system role is not supported. MessageSanitizer converts system messages to user with <system_instructions> wrapper.

Sourcegraph Cody Gateway may emit a mid-stream 'context deadline exceeded' error frame on HTTP 200 when upstream times out. GatewayErrorDetector detects this; SourcegraphChatClient sets finishReason='upstream_timeout'.
HTTP/2 RST_STREAM (CANCEL) errors occur with corporate proxies and Sourcegraph gateway timeouts. Plugin forces HTTP/1.1 via protocols(listOf(Protocol.HTTP_1_1)).
Partial XML tool call without gateway frame (truncation) is also promoted to upstream_timeout and triggers AgentLoop's retry+smaller-chunks recovery.
Force HTTP/1.1: both SourcegraphChatClient and SourcegraphCompletionsStreamClient set .protocols(listOf(Protocol.HTTP_1_1)) to avoid RST_STREAM errors.
SSE usage chunk is emitted in the FINAL delta frame (the one carrying finish_reason). Probe confirmed: saw_usage_chunk=true on all scenarios in streaming_lab_results.json.
Rate-limit headers parsed in priority order: retry-after > x-ratelimit-reset > ratelimit-reset. Handles both delta-seconds and Unix timestamp formats (SourcegraphChatClient.kt:581-595).

endpoints.summary: List all available LLM models on the Sourcegraph instance. Used by ModelCache to resolve the best available model at session start (priority: Anthropic Opus thinking > Opus > Sonnet). Result cached for 24 hours (TTL_MS in ModelCache).

params.name: Authorization
params.description: Sourcegraph token auth. Same scheme as chat/completions.

endpoints.provenance: SourcegraphChatClient.kt:83 (MODELS_PATH constant), SourcegraphChatClient.kt:151-179 (listModels impl), ModelCache.kt:22-58, ModelModels.kt:9-12 (ListModelsResponse DTO), research/2026-04-08-sourcegraph-api-surfaces.md Surface 1 table

verdict.classification: R-CORE: model discovery endpoint used at session start
verdict.reasoning: ModelCache.fetchModels calls this on first use (force=false) with a 24h TTL. Result drives ModelCache.pickBest() which picks the orchestrator model for the agent session. ModelInfo.id follows provider::apiVersion::modelId triple-colon format.

gotchas: Response shape is OpenAI ListModels: {data: [{id, owned_by, created}]}. ModelInfo extracts provider via substringBefore('::') and modelName via substringAfterLast('::').
ModelCache is a process-wide singleton (object). TTL is 24 hours. Call with force=true to bypass cache (e.g. Settings 'Refresh' button).
ModelCache.pickBest() preference order: Anthropic Opus thinking > Opus (any) > Sonnet (any) > any Anthropic > any. No hardcoded model IDs — selection is purely by tier heuristics on the live list.
On fetch failure, ModelCache returns cached models (possibly empty list) and logs a warn. Empty cached list means no model is selected and the agent cannot start.

endpoints.summary: Retrieve a single model by ID. Documented in the Sourcegraph OpenAPI spec (LLMService_retrieveModel) but not called by the plugin. Per-model metadata is obtained from /.api/modelconfig/supported-models.json instead.

params.name: modelId
params.description: Full model ID in provider::apiVersion::modelId format.

verdict.classification: R-INV: documented in OpenAPI spec, not called by plugin
verdict.reasoning: The plugin resolves per-model context windows and capabilities from /.api/modelconfig/supported-models.json (ModelCatalogService) rather than individual model GETs. This endpoint could be used for on-demand capability checks but is not needed today.

gotchas: modelId in the path must be URL-encoded if it contains '::' separators.

endpoints.summary: Cody-shape SSE streaming endpoint for image-bearing turns. Used by SourcegraphCompletionsStreamClient when BrainRouter detects any image content part in the message list. Distinct wire format from /.api/llm/chat/completions: uses 'speaker' instead of 'role', 'maxTokensToSample' instead of 'max_tokens', and returns SSE frames with 'deltaText' or 'completion' fields. The api-version query parameter is negotiated from /.api/client-config at runtime (default 8).

params.name: api-version
params.description: SSE frame shape version. Read from ModelCatalogService.getLatestStreamApiVersion() which reads latestSupportedCompletionsStreamAPIVersion from /.api/client-config. Default 8 (lowest version accepting image_url content parts). api-version >= 2 emits incremental deltaText; api-version 1 emits cumulative completion fields.

params.name: Authorization
params.description: Same token auth as other endpoints.

params.name: Accept
params.description: Must be text/event-stream to receive SSE response.

endpoints.requestBody: CompletionStreamRequest JSON: {model (required, triple-colon format), messages: [{speaker: 'human'|'assistant'|'system', content: [{type:'text',text:'...'} | {type:'image_url',image_url:{url:'data:image/png;base64,...'}}]}], maxTokensToSample (required), temperature (default 0.0), stream: true, topK: -1, topP: -1, tools: null (XML-in-content migration — tool defs are in system prompt, not on wire)}. NOTE: 'speaker' NOT 'role'. stray tool-role turns are coerced to 'human' by BrainRouter because this endpoint only accepts human/assistant/system.
endpoints.callSite: SourcegraphCompletionsStreamClient.kt:117 (chat method, builds URL with STREAM_PATH + ?api-version=N), SourcegraphCompletionsStreamClient.kt:195 (STREAM_PATH constant)
endpoints.provenance: SourcegraphCompletionsStreamClient.kt:29-58 (class KDoc), SourcegraphCompletionsStreamClient.kt:117-191 (chat impl), CompletionStreamDtos.kt:26-47 (CompletionStreamRequest), CompletionStreamDtos.kt:50-57 (StreamMessage with speaker field), research/2026-04-08-sourcegraph-api-surfaces.md Surface 2 (legacy SSE endpoint, independently confirms speaker lowercase + token auth)

verdict.classification: R-CORE: multimodal path; used for all image-bearing turns since Phase 6 (2026-05-05)
verdict.reasoning: BrainRouter.routeImageBearingTurn() uses this client when messages.any { it.hasImageParts() }. Post format_lab 2026-05-05 probe confirming tools on api-version=9, this client also forwarded tools; they were removed again in the 2026-05-13 XML-in-content migration.

gotchas: Wire format uses 'speaker' (not 'role') and 'maxTokensToSample' (not 'max_tokens'). Sending OpenAI-shaped fields to this endpoint will be silently ignored or cause errors.
Only three speaker values are accepted: 'human', 'assistant', 'system'. Tool-role messages must be coerced to 'human' (BrainRouter does this, preserving tool name in text body).
api-version >= 2 emits incremental deltaText frames; api-version 1 emits cumulative 'completion' field (callers must REPLACE, not append). CodyStreamSseParser handles both shapes.
End-of-stream is signaled by the FIRST of: 'event: done', 'data: [DONE]', or EOF. No [DONE] sentinel was observed in lab probes (saw_done_sentinel: false in streaming_lab_results.json).
Gateway in-band rejection is signaled via 'event: error' on HTTP 200 (not HTTP 4xx). BrainRouter renders this as a user-visible assistant bubble rather than a network error.
Unsupported image formats (HEIC, HEIF, BMP, TIFF, AVIF, SVG) result in an 'event: error' frame. BrainRouter surfaces: 'Sourcegraph rejected this attachment: ... Supported: PNG, JPEG, WebP.'
delta_tool_calls SSE frames are dropped by CodyStreamSseParser since XML-in-content migration 2026-05-13 (SourcegraphCompletionsStreamClient.kt:178).
The client is Sourcegraph-isolated: separate OkHttpClient, no shared connection pool, no CachingInterceptor (per project_sourcegraph_isolation.md).
Legacy speaker casing quirk: GraphQL completions use uppercase 'HUMAN'/'ASSISTANT', but the SSE endpoint requires lowercase 'human'/'assistant'. Only the SSE path is used by this plugin.

endpoints.summary: Full model catalog with per-model context windows, capabilities, tier overrides, and status. Used by ModelCatalogService to replace hard-coded 150K context assumptions with live, per-model values. Results cached for 1 hour. Drives vision capability detection, context-window sizing, and model status badge display in the model picker.
endpoints.callSite: ModelCatalogService.kt:152 (fetchCatalogFromGateway, URL built with CATALOG_PATH constant at line 195)
endpoints.provenance: ModelCatalogService.kt:195 (CATALOG_PATH constant), ModelCatalogService.kt:145-168 (fetchCatalogFromGateway impl), ModelCatalogDtos.kt:14-67 (ModelCatalog + Model + ContextWindow + TierOverride DTOs), ModelCatalogService.kt:26-47 (class KDoc)

verdict.classification: R-CORE: live model capability source; replaces all hard-coded model assumptions
verdict.reasoning: getContextWindow(modelRef, tier='enterprise') reads modelConfigAllTiers first (real value) then falls back to top-level contextWindow. Top-level is often a misleading minimum (e.g. 45K); per-tier override is the actual limit (e.g. enterprise 132K non-thinking). supportsVision() and supportsTools() also read from this catalog.

gotchas: Top-level contextWindow.maxInputTokens is often a low minimum (e.g. 45K). The REAL per-tier cap is in modelConfigAllTiers['enterprise'].contextWindow.maxInputTokens (e.g. 132K). Always read the per-tier value first.
TTL is 1 hour. Call getCatalog(force=true) to bypass (e.g. Settings 'Refresh capabilities' button).
modelConfigAllTiers is nullable — models without tier overrides use top-level contextWindow.
SharedCatalogHolder keys the catalog by sgUrl only; the tokenProvider half of the original cache key was removed (F-P6FU-2). One catalog instance per Sourcegraph base URL.

endpoints.summary: Sourcegraph instance feature flags and the latest supported completions stream API version. The latestSupportedCompletionsStreamAPIVersion field is used by ModelCatalogService.getLatestStreamApiVersion() to set the ?api-version=N query parameter on /.api/completions/stream calls. Results cached for 1 hour.
endpoints.callSite: ModelCatalogService.kt:171 (fetchClientConfigFromGateway, URL built with CLIENT_CONFIG_PATH constant at line 197)
endpoints.provenance: ModelCatalogService.kt:197 (CLIENT_CONFIG_PATH constant), ModelCatalogService.kt:170-191 (fetchClientConfigFromGateway impl), ModelCatalogService.kt:131-133 (getLatestStreamApiVersion, default 8), ClientConfigDto.kt:14-24 (ClientConfig DTO)

verdict.classification: R-CORE: api-version negotiation for image streaming path
verdict.reasoning: getLatestStreamApiVersion() defaults to 8 (DEFAULT_STREAM_API_VERSION) when client-config has not been loaded. v8 is the minimum version accepting image_url content parts. The value is appended as ?api-version=N to every /.api/completions/stream call.

gotchas: Default is 8 (not 9) when client-config fetch fails or has not run. v8 accepts image_url parts but may not support all streaming features of v9.
Both getCatalog and getClientConfig share the same 1-hour TTL and Mutex. refresh() fetches them in parallel via coroutineScope+async.
Plugin only reads latestSupportedCompletionsStreamAPIVersion from this response. All other flags (codyEnabled, chatEnabled, etc.) are fetched but not currently acted upon.

endpoints.summary: Repo-aware semantic code context retrieval. Returns ranked FileChunkContext results from the specified repositories for a natural-language query. Documented in the Sourcegraph OpenAPI spec (CodyService_context). Not called by the plugin today — the agent uses IDE-native search tools (search_code, find_references, etc.) for context gathering.
endpoints.requestBody: CodyContextRequest JSON: {query (required, natural-language), repos: [{name: string}] (RepoSpec array), codeResultsCount (0-100, default 15), textResultsCount (0-100, default 5), filePatterns: [glob], version: '1.0'|'2.0' (default '1.0')}
endpoints.provenance: research/2026-04-08-sourcegraph-api-surfaces.md Surface 1 table (CodyService_context operation ID) + Cody Context request/response schema section

verdict.classification: R-INV: documented, not adopted — could power future @repo mention feature
verdict.reasoning: The research doc explicitly notes this as a direct HTTP endpoint the agent could call for @repo mention features without going through the Cody JSON-RPC agent. Currently the plugin relies on IDE-native PSI + search tools instead.

gotchas: Response is results[] of FileChunkContext {blob (BlobInfo), startLine, endLine, chunkContent} with associated RepositoryInfo and CommitInfo.
RepoSpec.name must be the fully-qualified Sourcegraph repo name (e.g. github.com/org/repo), not a bare repo name.
version '2.0' may return richer metadata; '1.0' is the default and more widely supported.

endpoints.summary: Legacy Sourcegraph GraphQL completions endpoint (GetCompletions query). Uses uppercase speaker values (HUMAN/ASSISTANT) and returns a single non-streamed string in data.completions. Marked EXPERIMENTAL in the llmsp README. Not used by the plugin — the plugin targets the modern OpenAI-compatible REST surface only.
endpoints.requestBody: GraphQL query: {query: 'GetCompletions', variables: {messages: [{speaker: 'HUMAN'|'ASSISTANT', text: string}], temperature: Float, maxTokensToSample: Int, topK: Int, topP: Int}}. NOTE uppercase speaker, unlike the SSE endpoint.

verdict.reasoning: Surface 2 (llmsp) is marked EXPERIMENTAL with 'API may change at any point'. The plugin targets Surface 1 (OpenAI-compatible REST) exclusively. The GraphQL completions endpoint and its uppercase speaker values are documented here only for historical reference.

gotchas: Speaker casing is UPPERCASE for GraphQL (HUMAN/ASSISTANT) but lowercase for the SSE endpoint (human/assistant). This is a documented Sourcegraph-side quirk, not an llmsp bug.
Response is a single non-streamed string in data.completions — no SSE, no streaming.
This endpoint is on the same /.api/ path prefix as the modern API but is a completely different protocol (GraphQL vs REST).
